---
title: "01 — Data Exploration"
subtitle: "FIGARO MRIO + INCA Ecosystem Accounts"
author: "Nils Droste"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

```{julia}
#| label: setup
using Pkg
Pkg.activate(joinpath(@__DIR__, ".."))
# include("../src/EUSEEAShadow.jl")
# using .EUSEEAShadow
using CSV, DataFrames, Statistics, LinearAlgebra
```

## Overview

This notebook explores the raw and processed data before model estimation.
Sections:

1. FIGARO MRIO structure — sector and regional coverage
2. Ecosystem extent (CORINE Land Cover) — asset stock baseline
3. Ecosystem service flows (INCA) — R matrix construction
4. Marginal abatement costs — P_shadow identification

---

## 1. FIGARO MRIO Structure

```{julia}
#| label: figaro-check
proc_dir = joinpath(@__DIR__, "..", "data", "processed")

# Check which processed files exist
for f in ["figaro_A_2019.csv", "figaro_F_2019.csv", "figaro_x_2019.csv"]
    path = joinpath(proc_dir, f)
    status = isfile(path) ? "✓ found" : "✗ missing"
    println("$f: $status")
end
```

```{julia}
#| label: figaro-summary
#| eval: false  # enable after data download

A = Matrix(CSV.read(joinpath(proc_dir, "figaro_A_2019.csv"), DataFrame, header=false))
println("A matrix: $(size(A))")
println("Column sums (should be ≤ 1): ", round.(sum(A, dims=1)[1:5], digits=3))
```

---

## 2. Ecosystem Extent — CORINE Land Cover

```{julia}
#| label: clc-check
clc_path = joinpath(@__DIR__, "..", "data", "processed", "ecosystem_extent.csv")
if isfile(clc_path)
    extent = CSV.read(clc_path, DataFrame)
    println("Extent table: $(size(extent)) rows × cols")
    println(first(extent, 5))
else
    println("ecosystem_extent.csv not yet generated — run script 02.")
end
```

---

## 3. Ecosystem Service Flows — INCA R Matrix

```{julia}
#| label: inca-metadata
meta_path = joinpath(@__DIR__, "..", "data", "processed", "es_metadata.csv")
if isfile(meta_path)
    meta = CSV.read(meta_path, DataFrame)
    println(meta)
else
    println("es_metadata.csv not yet generated — run script 03.")
end
```

```{julia}
#| label: R-heatmap
#| eval: false  # enable after data available

using Plots
R = Matrix(CSV.read(joinpath(proc_dir, "R_matrix.csv"), DataFrame, header=false))
heatmap(log1p.(R), title="log(1 + R): ES intensity by sector",
        xlabel="Sector-region", ylabel="Ecosystem service",
        yticks=(1:size(R,1), meta.label))
```

---

## 4. Marginal Abatement Costs

```{julia}
#| label: mac-summary
if isfile(meta_path)
    meta = CSV.read(meta_path, DataFrame)
    sort!(meta, :mac_eur, rev=true)
    println(select(meta, :label, :unit, :mac_eur))
end
```

---

## 5. Toy Model Validation

```{julia}
#| label: toy-model
include(joinpath(@__DIR__, "..", "src", "EUSEEAShadow.jl"))
using .EUSEEAShadow

Z = [0.1 0.05 0.02 0.01;
     0.08 0.12 0.01 0.03;
     0.03 0.01 0.15 0.06;
     0.02 0.02 0.07 0.10]
x = [1.0, 1.2, 0.9, 1.1]
F = x .- vec(sum(Z, dims=2))
m = MRIOModel(Z, x, F, ["R1","R2"], ["S1","S2"])

L = leontief_inverse(m)
X = total_output(m)
println("Leontief inverse (L):")
display(round.(L, digits=3))
println("\nGross output X: ", round.(X, digits=3))
```

```{julia}
#| label: shadow-price-toy
R_toy = [0.1 0.05 0.08 0.03;
         0.02 0.04 0.01 0.06]
ea = EcosystemAccounts(R_toy, [10.0, 5.0], [0.5, 0.25],
                        ["Carbon seq.", "Water purif."], [80.0, 120.0])

V  = X .* 0.4
result = solve_shadow_prices(ea, m, V)

println("Shadow prices: ", result.P_shadow)
println("Passthrough (per unit final demand): ", round.(vec(result.passthrough), digits=4))
```
